functions.name: between
functions.description: Checks if a value is between a minimum and maximum value (inclusive).
functions.example_text: For example, between([age], 30, 40) would return true when [age] is 35.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The column or value to check

parameters.name: min_val
parameters.description: The column or value used as the minimum (inclusive)

parameters.name: max_val
parameters.description: The column or value used as the maximum (inclusive)

functions.returns: true if the value is between min and max (inclusive), otherwise false

functions.name: coalesce
functions.description: Returns the first non-empty value from a list of values.
functions.example_text: For example, coalesce([discount], 0) would return 0 when [discount] is empty.

parameters.name: *values
parameters.description: The columns or values to check in order

functions.returns: The first non-empty value, or null if all values are empty

functions.name: contains
functions.description: Checks if some text contains a specific pattern.
functions.example_text: For example, contains([product], "Laptop") would return true when [product] is "Laptop Pro 15".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or value to search in

parameters.name: search_for
parameters.description: The pattern to look for

functions.returns: true if the pattern is found in the text, otherwise false

functions.name: does_not_equal
functions.description: Checks if two values are different from each other.
functions.example_text: For example, does_not_equal([status], "cancelled") would return true when [status] is "shipped".

parameters.name: value1
parameters.description: The column or value to compare

parameters.name: value2
parameters.description: The column or value to compare it against

functions.returns: true if the values are different, false if they are the same

functions.name: equals
functions.description: Checks if two values are equal to each other.
functions.example_text: For example, equals([status], "shipped") would return true when [status] is "shipped".

parameters.name: value1
parameters.description: The column or value to compare

parameters.name: value2
parameters.description: The column or value to compare it against

functions.returns: true if both values are equal, otherwise false

functions.name: greatest
functions.description: Returns the largest value from a list of values.
functions.example_text: For example, greatest([price], 100) would return 100 when [price] is 79.99.

parameters.name: *values
parameters.description: The columns or values to compare

functions.name: ifnull
functions.description: Returns a default value if the input is empty.
functions.example_text: For example, ifnull([discount], 0) would return 0 when [discount] is empty.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The column or value to check

parameters.name: default
parameters.description: The column or value to return if the first value is empty

functions.returns: The original value if not empty, otherwise the default value

functions.name: is_empty
functions.description: Checks if a value is empty (missing).
functions.example_text: For example, is_empty([email]) would return true when [email] is empty.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The column to check

functions.returns: true if the value is empty, otherwise false

functions.name: is_not_empty
functions.description: Checks if a value contains something (is not missing).
functions.example_text: For example, is_not_empty([discount]) would return false when [discount] is empty.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The column to check

functions.returns: true if the value contains something, false if it is empty

functions.name: is_string
functions.description: Checks if a literal value is text (a string). This works on fixed values, not on column references.
functions.example_text: For example, is_string("hello") would return true.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to check

functions.returns: true if the value is text, otherwise false

functions.name: least
functions.description: Returns the smallest value from a list of values.
functions.example_text: For example, least([price], 100) would return 100 when [price] is 249.99.

parameters.name: *values
parameters.description: The columns or values to compare

functions.name: nullif
functions.description: Returns null if the two values are equal, otherwise returns the first value.
functions.example_text: For example, nullif([status], "cancelled") would return null when [status] is "cancelled".

parameters.name: value1
parameters.description: The column or value to return

parameters.name: value2
parameters.description: The value to compare against

functions.returns: null if both are equal, otherwise the first value

functions.name: nvl
functions.description: Returns a default value if the input is empty (alias for ifnull).
functions.example_text: For example, nvl([email], "no email") would return "no email" when [email] is empty.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The column or value to check

parameters.name: default
parameters.description: The column or value to return if the first value is empty

functions.returns: The original value if not empty, otherwise the default value

functions.name: concat
functions.description: Combines multiple text values into a single text.
functions.example_text: For example, concat([first_name], " ", [last_name]) would return "John Doe" when [first_name] is "John" and [last_name] is "Doe".

parameters.name: *text_parts
parameters.description: The columns or texts you want to combine

functions.name: count_match
functions.description: Counts how many times a pattern appears in text.
functions.example_text: For example, count_match([email], ".") would return 2 when [email] is "john.doe@company.com".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to search in

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: The pattern to search for

functions.name: ends_with
functions.description: Checks if text ends with a specific suffix.
functions.example_text: For example, ends_with([email], ".com") would return true when [email] is "john.doe@company.com".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to check

parameters.name: suffix
parameters.description: The suffix to look for at the end

functions.returns: True if the text ends with the suffix, False otherwise

functions.name: find_position
functions.description: Finds the position of a substring within text (0-based index).
functions.example_text: For example, find_position([order_id], "-") would return 3 when [order_id] is "ORD-0001".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to search in

parameters.name: sub
parameters.description: The substring to find the position of

functions.returns: The position of the substring, or null if it is not found

functions.name: left
functions.description: Gets a specified number of characters from the beginning of text.
functions.example_text: For example, left([order_id], 3) would return "ORD" when [order_id] is "ORD-0001".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to extract from

parameters.name: num_chars
parameters.description: How many characters to take from the beginning

functions.name: left_trim
functions.description: Removes spaces from the beginning of text.
functions.example_text: For example, left_trim([city]) would return "Amsterdam  " when [city] is "  Amsterdam  ".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text you want to trim

functions.name: length
functions.description: Counts the number of characters in text.
functions.example_text: For example, length([city]) would return 9 when [city] is "Amsterdam".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to measure

functions.name: lowercase
functions.description: Converts text to all lowercase.
functions.example_text: For example, lowercase([department]) would return "engineering" when [department] is "Engineering".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to convert

functions.name: mid
functions.description: Extracts a portion of text from the middle starting at a specified position.
functions.example_text: For example, mid([order_id], 4, 4) would return "0001" when [order_id] is "ORD-0001".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to extract from

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The starting position (0-based index)

parameters.name: num_chars
parameters.description: How many characters to extract

functions.name: pad_left
functions.description: Adds characters to the beginning of text to reach a specific length.
functions.example_text: For example, pad_left([order_id], 12, "0") would return "0000ORD-0001" when [order_id] is "ORD-0001".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text you want to pad

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: How long you want the final text to be

parameters.name: pad_character
parameters.description: What character to use for padding (default is a space)

functions.name: pad_right
functions.description: Adds characters to the end of text to reach a specific length.
functions.example_text: For example, pad_right([order_id], 12, "0") would return "ORD-00010000" when [order_id] is "ORD-0001".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text you want to pad

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: How long you want the final text to be

parameters.name: pad_character
parameters.description: What character to use for padding (default is a space)

functions.name: repeat
functions.description: Repeats text a specified number of times.
functions.example_text: For example, repeat([first_name], 2) would return "JohnJohn" when [first_name] is "John".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to repeat

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: How many times to repeat the text

functions.name: replace
functions.description: Replaces specific text with different text.
functions.example_text: For example, replace([email], ".com", ".org") would return "john.doe@company.org" when [email] is "john.doe@company.com".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text where replacements will be made

parameters.name: find_text
parameters.description: The text you want to find and replace

parameters.name: replace_with
parameters.description: The new text that will replace the found text

functions.name: reverse
functions.description: Reverses the characters in text.
functions.example_text: For example, reverse([first_name]) would return "nhoJ" when [first_name] is "John".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to reverse

functions.name: right
functions.description: Gets a specified number of characters from the end of text.
functions.example_text: For example, right([order_id], 4) would return "0001" when [order_id] is "ORD-0001".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to extract from

parameters.name: num_chars
parameters.description: How many characters to take from the end

functions.name: right_trim
functions.description: Removes spaces from the end of text.
functions.example_text: For example, right_trim([city]) would return "Amsterdam" when [city] is "Amsterdam  ".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text you want to trim

functions.name: split
functions.description: Splits text into a list using a delimiter.
functions.example_text: For example, split([product], " ") would return ["Laptop", "Pro"] when [product] is "Laptop Pro".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to split

parameters.name: delimiter
parameters.description: The character(s) to split on

functions.name: starts_with
functions.description: Checks if text starts with a specific prefix.
functions.example_text: For example, starts_with([order_id], "ORD") would return true when [order_id] is "ORD-0001".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to check

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: The prefix to look for at the start

functions.returns: True if the text starts with the prefix, False otherwise

functions.name: string_similarity
functions.description: Measures how similar two texts are to each other, on a scale of 0 to 1. A value of 1 means the texts are identical, while 0 means they are completely different.
functions.example_text: For example, string_similarity([first_name], [last_name], "levenshtein") would return 0.4 when [first_name] is "John" and [last_name] is "Jones".

parameters.name: text1
parameters.description: The first column or text to compare

parameters.name: text2
parameters.description: The second column or text to compare

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: Which comparison method to use (default is 'levenshtein')

functions.name: substring
functions.description: Extracts a portion of text starting at a specified position (alias for mid).
functions.example_text: For example, substring([order_id], 0, 3) would return "ORD" when [order_id] is "ORD-0001".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to extract from

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The starting position (0-based index)

parameters.name: num_chars
parameters.description: How many characters to extract

functions.name: titlecase
functions.description: Converts Text To Title Case, Where Each Word Is Capitalized.
functions.example_text: For example, titlecase([event]) would return "Tech Conference" when [event] is "tech conference".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to convert

functions.name: trim
functions.description: Removes spaces from both the beginning and end of text.
functions.example_text: For example, trim([city]) would return "Amsterdam" when [city] is "  Amsterdam  ".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text you want to trim

functions.name: uppercase
functions.description: Converts text to ALL UPPERCASE.
functions.example_text: For example, uppercase([city]) would return "AMSTERDAM" when [city] is "Amsterdam".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The column or text to convert

functions.name: abs
functions.description: Returns the absolute value of each value in a column (removes the negative sign).
functions.example_text: For example, abs([price]) would return 5.0 when [price] is -5.0.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to get the absolute value of

functions.returns: The non-negative version of the value

functions.name: acos
functions.description: Calculates the arccosine (inverse cosine) of each value in a column.
functions.example_text: For example, acos([ratio]) would return 0.0 when [ratio] is 1.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to take the arccosine of (values between -1 and 1)

functions.returns: The angle in radians whose cosine equals the value

functions.name: asin
functions.description: Calculates the arcsine (inverse sine) of each value in a column.
functions.example_text: For example, asin([ratio]) would return 0.0 when [ratio] is 0.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to take the arcsine of (values between -1 and 1)

functions.returns: The angle in radians whose sine equals the value

functions.name: atan
functions.description: Calculates the arctangent (inverse tangent) of each value in a column.
functions.example_text: For example, atan([ratio]) would return 0.0 when [ratio] is 0.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to take the arctangent of

functions.returns: The angle in radians whose tangent equals the value

functions.name: ceil
functions.description: Rounds each value in a column up to the nearest whole number.
functions.example_text: For example, ceil([price]) would return 5.0 when [price] is 4.2.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to round up

functions.returns: The value rounded up to a whole number

functions.name: cos
functions.description: Calculates the cosine of each value in a column, where values are angles in radians.
functions.example_text: For example, cos([angle]) would return 1.0 when [angle] is 0.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value with angles in radians

functions.name: exp
functions.description: Calculates e raised to the power of each value in a column.
functions.example_text: For example, exp([discount]) would return 1.0 when [discount] is 0.0.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to use as the exponent of e

functions.returns: The result of e raised to the value

functions.name: floor
functions.description: Rounds each value in a column down to the nearest whole number.
functions.example_text: For example, floor([price]) would return 4.0 when [price] is 4.7.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to round down

functions.returns: The value rounded down to a whole number

functions.name: log
functions.description: Calculates the natural logarithm (base e) of each value in a column.
functions.example_text: For example, log([price]) would return 0.0 when [price] is 1.0.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to take the natural logarithm of

functions.returns: The natural logarithm of the value

functions.name: log10
functions.description: Calculates the base-10 logarithm of each value in a column.
functions.example_text: For example, log10([salary]) would return 4.0 when [salary] is 10000.0.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to take the base-10 logarithm of

functions.returns: The base-10 logarithm of the value

functions.name: log2
functions.description: Calculates the base-2 logarithm of each value in a column.
functions.example_text: For example, log2([price]) would return 3.0 when [price] is 8.0.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to take the base-2 logarithm of

functions.returns: The base-2 logarithm of the value

functions.name: mod
functions.description: Calculates the remainder after dividing each value in a column (modulo).
functions.example_text: For example, mod([quantity], 3) would return 1 when [quantity] is 10.

parameters.name: dividend
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to be divided

parameters.name: divisor
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The number to divide by

functions.name: negation
functions.description: Flips the sign of each value in a column (positive becomes negative, negative becomes positive).
functions.example_text: For example, negation([price]) would return -19.99 when [price] is 19.99.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to flip the sign of

functions.returns: The value with its sign flipped

functions.name: negative
functions.description: Returns the constant value -1; it takes no arguments.
functions.example_text: For example, negative() would return -1.

functions.name: pow
functions.description: Raises each value in a column to a power (alias for power).
functions.example_text: For example, pow([quantity], 2) would return 16 when [quantity] is 4.

parameters.name: base
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to raise

parameters.name: exponent
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The power to raise the base to

functions.returns: The result of base raised to the power of exponent

functions.name: power
functions.description: Raises each value in a column to a power.
functions.example_text: For example, power([quantity], 2) would return 9 when [quantity] is 3.

parameters.name: base
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to raise

parameters.name: exponent
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The power to raise the base to

functions.returns: The result of base raised to the power of exponent

functions.name: random_int
functions.description: Generates a random whole number for each row, from min_value (inclusive) up to max_value (exclusive).
functions.example_text: For example, random_int(1, 100) would return a random value such as 57.

parameters.name: min_value
parameters.description: The smallest possible number to generate (default is 0)

parameters.name: max_value
parameters.description: The upper bound, which is never reached (default is 2)

functions.returns: A random whole number for each row in the given range

functions.name: round
functions.description: Rounds each value in a column to a specified number of decimal places.
functions.example_text: For example, round([price], 2) would return 19.99 when [price] is 19.987.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to round

parameters.name: decimal_places
parameters.description: How many decimal places to keep (default is 0)

functions.name: sign
functions.description: Returns the sign of each value in a column (-1, 0, or 1).
functions.example_text: For example, sign([quantity]) would return -1 when [quantity] is -2.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to check

functions.returns: 1 if negative, 0 if zero, 1 if positive

functions.name: sin
functions.description: Calculates the sine of each value in a column, where values are angles in radians.
functions.example_text: For example, sin([angle]) would return 0.0 when [angle] is 0.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value with angles in radians

functions.name: sqrt
functions.description: Calculates the square root of each value in a column.
functions.example_text: For example, sqrt([price]) would return 3.0 when [price] is 9.0.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to take the square root of

functions.returns: The square root of the value

functions.name: tan
functions.description: Calculates the tangent of each value in a column, where values are angles in radians.
functions.example_text: For example, tan([angle]) would return 0.0 when [angle] is 0.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value with angles in radians

functions.name: tanh
functions.description: Calculates the hyperbolic tangent of each value in a column; the result is always between -1 and 1.
functions.example_text: For example, tanh([discount]) would return 0.0 when [discount] is 0.0.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128, Float32, Int64})
parameters.description: The column or value to take the hyperbolic tangent of

functions.returns: The hyperbolic tangent of the value

functions.name: add_days
functions.description: Adds a number of days to a date.
functions.example_text: For example, add_days([hire_date], 5) would return 2021-03-20 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to add days to

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: How many days to add

functions.name: add_hours
functions.description: Adds a number of hours to a datetime.
functions.example_text: For example, add_hours([order_date], 3) would return 2024-01-15 13:30:00 when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:00.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The datetime column or expression to add hours to

parameters.name: hours
parameters.description: How many hours to add

functions.name: add_minutes
functions.description: Adds a number of minutes to a datetime.
functions.example_text: For example, add_minutes([order_date], 15) would return 2024-01-15 10:45:00 when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:00.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The datetime column or expression to add minutes to

parameters.name: minutes
parameters.description: How many minutes to add

functions.name: add_months
functions.description: Adds a number of months to a date.
functions.example_text: For example, add_months([hire_date], 2) would return 2021-05-15 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to add months to

parameters.name: months
parameters.description: How many months to add

functions.name: add_seconds
functions.description: Adds a number of seconds to a datetime.
functions.example_text: For example, add_seconds([order_date], 30) would return 2024-01-15 10:30:30 when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:00.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The datetime column or expression to add seconds to

parameters.name: seconds
parameters.description: How many seconds to add

functions.name: add_weeks
functions.description: Adds a number of weeks to a date.
functions.example_text: For example, add_weeks([hire_date], 2) would return 2021-03-29 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to add weeks to

parameters.name: weeks
parameters.description: How many weeks to add

functions.name: add_years
functions.description: Adds a number of years to a date.
functions.example_text: For example, add_years([hire_date], 1) would return 2022-03-15 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to add years to

parameters.name: years
parameters.description: How many years to add

functions.name: date_diff_days
functions.description: Calculates the number of days between two dates (the first minus the second).
functions.example_text: For example, date_diff_days(today(), [hire_date]) would return 30 when [hire_date] is 30 days before today.

parameters.name: date1
parameters.description: The date column or expression to subtract from

parameters.name: date2
parameters.description: The date column or expression to subtract

functions.returns: The number of days between the two dates

functions.name: date_trim
functions.description: Removes the smaller parts of a date or time.
functions.example_text: For example, date_trim([order_date], "day") would return 2024-01-15 00:00:00 when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:45.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The datetime column or expression to trim

parameters.name: part
parameters.description: Which part to keep ('year', 'month', 'day', 'hour', 'minute', or 'second')

functions.name: date_truncate
functions.description: Rounds a date down to the nearest specified unit.
functions.example_text: For example, date_truncate([order_date], "1d") would return 2024-01-15 00:00:00 when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:45.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The datetime column or expression to truncate

parameters.name: truncate_by
parameters.description: The time unit to round down to (like "1d", "2h", "15m")

functions.name: datetime_diff_nanoseconds
functions.description: Calculates the number of nanoseconds between two datetimes (the first minus the second), for very precise time measurements.
functions.example_text: For example, datetime_diff_nanoseconds([end], [start]) would return 1000000000 when [start] is 2024-01-15 10:00:00 and [end] is 2024-01-15 10:00:01.

parameters.name: date1
parameters.description: The datetime column or expression to subtract from

parameters.name: date2
parameters.description: The datetime column or expression to subtract

functions.returns: The number of nanoseconds between the two datetimes

functions.name: datetime_diff_seconds
functions.description: Calculates the number of seconds between two datetimes (the first minus the second).
functions.example_text: For example, datetime_diff_seconds([end], [start]) would return 3600 when [start] is 2024-01-15 10:00:00 and [end] is 2024-01-15 11:00:00.

parameters.name: date1
parameters.description: The datetime column or expression to subtract from

parameters.name: date2
parameters.description: The datetime column or expression to subtract

functions.returns: The number of seconds between the two datetimes

functions.name: day
functions.description: Gets the day of the month from a date.
functions.example_text: For example, day([hire_date]) would return 15 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to extract the day from

functions.returns: The day of the month as a number (1-31)

functions.name: dayofweek
functions.description: Gets the day of the week from a date (alias for weekday, 1=Monday, 7=Sunday).
functions.example_text: For example, dayofweek([hire_date]) would return 1 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15, a Monday.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to extract the day of week from

functions.returns: The day of the week as a number (1-7)

functions.name: dayofyear
functions.description: Gets the day of the year from a date (1-366).
functions.example_text: For example, dayofyear([hire_date]) would return 74 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to extract the day of year from

functions.returns: The day of the year as a number (1-366)

functions.name: end_of_month
functions.description: Gets the last day of the month for a given date.
functions.example_text: For example, end_of_month([hire_date]) would return 2021-03-31 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to get the end of month for

functions.returns: The last day of the month

functions.name: format_date
functions.description: Formats a date as text using a specified format.
functions.example_text: For example, format_date([hire_date], "%B %d, %Y") would return "March 15, 2021" when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to format

parameters.name: date_format
parameters.description: The output format string (default is year-month-day)

functions.name: hour
functions.description: Gets the hour from a datetime.
functions.example_text: For example, hour([order_date]) would return 10 when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:45.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The datetime column or expression to extract the hour from

functions.name: minute
functions.description: Gets the minute from a datetime.
functions.example_text: For example, minute([order_date]) would return 30 when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:45.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The datetime column or expression to extract the minute from

functions.name: month
functions.description: Gets the month from a date.
functions.example_text: For example, month([hire_date]) would return 3 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to extract the month from

functions.name: now
functions.description: Gets the current date and time.
functions.example_text: For example, now() would return the current date and time.

functions.name: quarter
functions.description: Gets the quarter from a date (1-4).
functions.example_text: For example, quarter([hire_date]) would return 1 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to extract the quarter from

functions.name: second
functions.description: Gets the second from a datetime.
functions.example_text: For example, second([order_date]) would return 45 when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:45.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The datetime column or expression to extract the second from

functions.name: start_of_month
functions.description: Gets the first day of the month for a given date.
functions.example_text: For example, start_of_month([hire_date]) would return 2021-03-01 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to get the start of month for

functions.returns: The first day of the month

functions.name: today
functions.description: Gets the current date.
functions.example_text: For example, today() would return the current date.

functions.name: week
functions.description: Gets the ISO week number from a date (1-53).
functions.example_text: For example, week([hire_date]) would return 11 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to extract the week from

functions.returns: The week number as a number (1-53)

functions.name: weekday
functions.description: Gets the day of the week from a date (1=Monday, 7=Sunday).
functions.example_text: For example, weekday([hire_date]) would return 1 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15, a Monday.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to extract the weekday from

functions.returns: The day of the week as a number (1-7)

functions.name: year
functions.description: Gets the year from a date.
functions.example_text: For example, year([hire_date]) would return 2021 when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date column or expression to extract the year from

functions.name: to_boolean
functions.description: Converts a column or value to true or false. Non-zero numbers and text like "true", "yes", "t" or "y" become true; zero and text like "false", "no", "f" or "n" become false.
functions.example_text: For example, to_boolean([quantity]) would return true when [quantity] is 1.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The column or value to convert to a boolean

functions.name: to_date
functions.description: Parses a text column or value into a date.
functions.example_text: For example, to_date("2023-05-15") would return the date 2023-05-15.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text column or value to parse as a date

parameters.name: date_format
parameters.description: How to interpret the date text (default is "%Y-%m-%d")

functions.name: to_datetime
functions.description: Parses a text column or value into a datetime.
functions.example_text: For example, to_datetime("2023-05-15 14:30:00") would return the datetime 2023-05-15 14:30:00.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The text column or value to parse as a datetime

parameters.name: date_format
parameters.description: How to interpret the datetime text (default is "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")

functions.name: to_decimal
functions.description: Converts a column or value to a decimal number rounded to a fixed number of decimal places.
functions.example_text: For example, to_decimal([price], 2) would return 19.99 when [price] is 19.987.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The column or value to convert to a decimal

parameters.name: precision
parameters.description: How many decimal places to keep (default is None, which keeps all decimal places)

functions.name: to_float
functions.description: Converts a column or value to a number with decimal places.
functions.example_text: For example, to_float([quantity]) would return 3.0 when [quantity] is 3.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The column or value to convert to a floating-point number

functions.name: to_integer
functions.description: Converts a column or value to a whole number, truncating any decimal places.
functions.example_text: For example, to_integer([price]) would return 19 when [price] is 19.99.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The column or value to convert to an integer

functions.returns: The integer value (decimal places are truncated)

functions.name: to_number
functions.description: Converts a column or value to a number (same as to_float).
functions.example_text: For example, to_number([quantity]) would return 3.0 when [quantity] is 3.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The column or value to convert to a number

functions.name: to_string
functions.description: Converts a column or value to text.
functions.example_text: For example, to_string([age]) would return "30" when [age] is 30.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The column or value to convert to text
